codeReview.summary: The fix addresses the original leakage bug. In src/features/defense.py, current-season fielding and framing snapshots no longer backfill rolling histories when they lack dates; the code now only uses dated current-season observations for 30/60 windows and falls back to prior-season or league baselines when no safe history exists. The updated tests cover both the prior-season fallback and the no-history league-average path, and I did not find a new blocking issue in the reviewed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Workers on this repo still have to deviate from the startup procedure on Windows because the feature-worker guidance does not point them to the already-documented Windows-specific init path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The reviewed worker reported skillFeedback.followedProcedure = false in C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-19T19-13-17-089Z__fix-t11-defense-dated-inputs__8442c7e1-7314-4588-9b3e-45ebe0611cb6.json after .factory/init.sh failed on Windows. C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\skills\feature-worker\SKILL.md:8,41 still only references generic worker-base startup and non-Windows pytest commands, while the repo-specific fallback already exists at C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\skills\data-worker\SKILL.md:19-24, C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\environment.md:59-60, and C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\services.yaml:7.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the original failed feature, its prior scrutiny report, the fix handoff/transcript skeleton, and both diffs. The fix removes the unsafe current-season snapshot fallback that caused the original blocking leakage issue and replaces it with safe prior-season or league baselines when dated defense history is unavailable, without introducing a new blocking problem.
